Which two commands log you out from user EXEC?
cards.back: `exit` and `logout` — both close the EXEC session.

cards.front: Can you run `configure terminal` from user EXEC mode?
cards.back: No — `configure` only appears in privileged EXEC (`#`). Must `enable` first.

cards.front: Which global config command turns CDP off globally?
Which global config command turns LLDP on globally?
Which command from global config undoes any setting?
Why does `Device1(config)#enable` exist if `enable` also exists in user EXEC?
cards.back: Different command. In user EXEC, `enable` ENTERS privileged mode. In global config, `enable <secret|password>` SETS the enable password.

cards.front: Different command. In user EXEC, `enable` ENTERS privileged mode. In global config, `enable <secret|password>` SETS the enable password.
